theme: auspicious tatami mat layout of a court
title: One Broken Mat: Auspicious Tatami Court Layout
seed_problem: tatami-tiling: cover a room with 1x2 mats (+1 forced 1x1) so no four mat corners meet at a point
mutation: composite: the exact-cover decision problem is opened into a packing/arrangement optimum -- a partial mat layout is scored (never forced to fully tile), and the objective couples three mechanisms into one number: local orientation-mix entropy over sliding windows, a T-junction motif fraction gated by the same no-four-corner feasibility rule, and a 180-degree mirror-symmetry bonus over the seam graph. The single required 1x1 mat is a parity defect: its placement is a free choice that determines which globally symmetric seam graphs are even reachable, so the placement decision precedes and controls the rest of the construction.
strategies: trivial: cover only even rows with a fixed left-to-right pairing, drop the 1x1 mat on the first free odd-row cell -- reproduces the checker's own baseline

greedy (running-bond / brick): dense fixed-phase horizontal-only offset tiling covering almost the whole court, satisfies the no-four-corner rule trivially by row-parity offset alone, drops the 1x1 mat wherever the scan first stalls -- zero orientation entropy, chance-level (~0.5) mirror symmetry

strong (insight): reserve the court's true centre for the 1x1 defect FIRST, partition the rest into mirror-paired blocks, build one block of each mirror pair with a running-bond pattern (alternating horizontal/vertical by block parity) and derive its partner by reflecting every mat through the centre -- trades some coverage for high orientation entropy and near-perfect (~0.95+) mirror symmetry

beyond strong: mix orientations WITHIN a block (not just between blocks) using a locally-verified safe stagger, or search over which cell is the parity defect rather than always taking the geometric centre, to close the remaining coverage/entropy trade-off
